where: XIII-2 (proof of Théorème 2.1, paragraph following the proof of Lemme 2.0)
before: Donc comme H ⊃ C, d'où \mathfrak{h} \supset \mathfrak{c}, il s'ensuit que \mathfrak{h} \subset \mathfrak{g}^T
after: unchanged (no edit made to fr JSON or transcribed HTML)
evidence: PDF p. 171 (pdftotext) prints "Donc comme H ⊃ C, d'où h ⊃ n, il s'ensuit que h ⊂ g^T": the printed original has \mathfrak{n} where the fr transcription has \mathfrak{c}. The transcription's \mathfrak{c} is the mathematically correct reading (H ⊃ C gives h ⊃ c), i.e. a silent fix of an original misprint, so it was left as is; the neighbouring '⊂' (arguably also a misprint for '⊃' in the original edition) is printed in the PDF exactly as transcribed and was therefore kept.
